title: ChatGPT Introduces $100/Month Pro Plan, Filling Subscription Gap
summary: OpenAI has launched a new $100/month ChatGPT Pro plan, addressing user demand for a mid-tier option. The move bridges the gap between the $20 and $200 monthly plans.
body:
OpenAI has announced a new $100/month ChatGPT Pro plan, responding to longstanding user requests for a more affordable premium option. The subscription, which includes priority access to new features and faster response times, slots between the existing $20/month basic plan and the $200/month enterprise offering.

The new plan is a strategic move to capture power users who need more than the basic plan but aren't ready for the high-end enterprise tier. It also reflects OpenAI's efforts to diversify its revenue streams as competition in the AI space heats up. The pricing structure now offers a more gradual progression, potentially appealing to a broader range of users.

Reactions to the new plan have been largely positive, with users praising the more accessible pricing. However, some enterprise customers may question whether the $200 plan still offers enough additional value to justify the significant price jump. OpenAI will need to continue innovating to maintain its market lead, and this new plan could be a key part of that strategy.
twitterThread:
🚀 ChatGPT just dropped a $100/month Pro plan.

Finally, a middle ground between $20 and $200.

Here's why this matters:

1. Power users wanted this. For years. OpenAI listened.
2. $100 is the sweet spot for professionals who need more than basic but aren't enterprise.
3. This is OpenAI's move to lock in users before competitors swoop in.

The wild part?

The $200 plan now looks overpriced. What's in that tier that justifies DOUBLE the cost?

OpenAI better have answers.

Let that sink in.

OpenAI is playing the long game. They're not just selling AI. They're selling stickiness.

This changes everything for...

Competitors. Mistral, Anthropic, and the rest just got a wake-up call.

OpenAI is raising the bar on pricing AND features.

The AI wars are heating up.
